Mitigation 1: Human radiologist review required for all recommendations
All AI predictions are presented as decision-support only. A qualified radiologist
must review and sign off on every AI recommendation before it is included in the
clinical record. The radiologist can override the AI prediction at any time.

Mitigation 2: Input validation — image quality score check
Before inference, each DICOM image is assessed by a quality scoring module that
checks for motion artifact, exposure levels, and field-of-view completeness.
Images with a quality score below 0.6 are rejected with an "unprocessable image"
response and flagged for manual radiologist review.

Mitigation 3: Confidence threshold — reject low-confidence predictions
Predictions with confidence score below 0.70 are not shown to the requesting
physician. Instead, a "low confidence — radiologist review required" alert is
sent. This reduces the risk of overconfident wrong predictions reaching clinicians.

Mitigation 4: Monthly performance evaluation against holdout set
On the first Monday of each month, the model is automatically evaluated against a
1,000-image internal holdout set. If accuracy drops more than 2 percentage points
from the baseline, an automated alert is sent to the ML engineering team and
deployment is paused pending investigation.
